reason: Backend API, services, Prisma schema, migrations, and tests are required for review-center data management.
New and changed route handlers must follow the project API envelope and error conventions.
The feature adds review/persona/biography endpoints and must follow App Router route handler patterns.
Queries span Persona, Profile, BiographyRecord, Mention, Relationship, and Chapter data.
The PRD allows a new persisted chapter-review status table, requiring a Prisma migration.
Service and route tests are required for CRUD, deletion preview, and chapter verification state.
Cross-layer contracts need strict TypeScript and Prisma type safety.
The review center UI adds new tabs, workbench components, and forms.
Chapter workbench and role-management UI should be decomposed into maintainable components.
The UI must clearly display and edit character events using existing design-system patterns.
Client state, forms, effects, and data refresh flows must follow React conventions.
Frontend service contracts and component props must stay typed across API boundaries.
The new review UI needs focused tests and quality checks.
Shared cross-layer standards apply to API contracts and validation types.
New route payloads should use Zod validation with inferred TypeScript contracts where appropriate.
General cross-cutting project guidance for implementation.
This feature spans UI, API routes, services, database, and tests.
Keep review UI, service modules, route handlers, and data access responsibilities separated.
